fields.narrative_intro: *An exhausted person and a sheepish person discuss Claude Opus 4.8 at a gas station in the desert.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "Anthropic rewrote 750,000 lines of code in 11 days."
SHEEPISH PERSON: "Impressive. Who did the work?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "Hundreds of Opus 4.8 agents. In parallel."
SHEEPISH PERSON: "And the previous version couldn't do that?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "The previous version just... quietly watched bad code pass through."
SHEEPISH PERSON: "It was comfortable with that?"
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The sheepish person stares at a single gas pump displaying $25/M output tokens. A tumbleweed rolls past. Neither person blinks.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "Anthropic's words: 'quite comfortable.'"
SHEEPISH PERSON: "They shipped that."
fields.panel6:
[button]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "At the same price."
[Visual: The gas station's price board reads: REGULAR $5/M · PREMIUM $25/M · SELF-AWARE: ∞]

narrative_summary: The joke lands on Anthropic's deadpan admission that its previous flagship model was 'quite comfortable' letting bad code slide through, reframed as a product feature calmly discussed at a desert gas station where the price board echoes the token pricing.
rationale: Candidate 1 earns its tie-breaking edge through the panel 6 price board gag — 'SELF-AWARE: ∞' — which is the only closing beat that adds a genuinely new idea rather than restating the bug-tolerance premise, and the desert gas station token-pricing visual metaphor is tightest here. The deadpan silence of panel 4 also does real structural work that the escalation and sight-gag versions slightly undercut with over-explanation.
place: at a gas station in the desert
hat_pair: hard hat with a tiny disco ball glued to the top
